summary: A Jest test in `test/config/get.ts` fails because the assertion expects the registry URL without a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`) but the code returns the URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`). This causes the test suite to exit with status 1 and the CI job to fail.
root_cause: The test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` at `test/config/get.ts:32` uses `toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com')` but the actual output is `'https://my-org.registry.example.com/'` (with a trailing slash), indicating the registry URL normalization behavior changed or the test expectation is too strict.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote: ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the specific failing test case.
Shows what the test assertion expected — URL without trailing slash.
Shows what the code actually returned — URL with trailing slash, causing the mismatch.
Pinpoints the exact assertion line in the test file that failed.
Stack trace confirms the failure location is test/config/get.ts line 32.
Confirms exactly one test failed in the entire suite.
Confirms the test runner exited with a non-zero status, causing the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Either update the test expectation at `test/config/get.ts:32` to accept a trailing slash (`toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com/')`) if the new behavior is intentional, or fix the registry URL normalization code to strip trailing slashes before returning the value to match the original expected behavior.
